title: Rich Smoky Indian Cacao Gratin
description: A salty, molten Indian dish featuring cacao, beurre-monte, and Rajasthani spices, served on a raclette grill for a hangover cure. Perfect for paleo enthusiasts and those seeking a unique breakfast option.
instructions: Melt 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add 1 cup of cacao nibs and cook, stirring frequently, for 5 minutes.
Add 1 cup of almond milk, 1 cup of water, and 2 tablespoons of ghee, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of ground cumin, 1 teaspoon of ground coriander, and 1/2 teaspoon of cayenne pepper.
In a small bowl, whip 1 cup of heavy cream until stiff peaks form. Set aside.

To assemble the gratin, place a layer of cacao mixture on the raclette grill, followed by a layer of beurre-monte, and finally a layer of whipped cream. Repeat this process two more times, ending with a layer of whipped cream on top.
Place the raclette grill under the broiler and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the top layer of cream is golden brown and the dish is hot and bubbly.
Remove the gratin from the grill and serve immediately, garnished with cacao nibs and edible flowers if desired.
